review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Titan is beautiful but ticks a little slow!
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
- Titan has a reputed name in the society and being part of such company adds value. 
- Truly one of the best work campuses in Bangalore.
- You find a lot of inspiring leaders in Business teams and if you get a chance to interact with them, nothing like it.
- Great company to settle down.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. People - Titan hires people in different ways 1. Campus placement - where most talented people join. 2. Agencies - The people who join full-time through agency kill others' productivity and time. I've interacted with various people who joined through the agency - they have poor communication skills, and poor hard/soft skills and a lot of time is spent teaching them basics even a little bit of English. When you give this as a concern to your manager, he/she often says they will be useful to fill the current gaps. (Wait, are you looking to fill the current gaps with low-quality talents or invest time in someone who can help the company achieve its outcomes?) All these people would also interact with other talented teams in the future. It is really important for Titan to raise their recruitment standard so that team collaboration happens well. I hope the HR and leadership teams actually look into the quality of recruitment. 

Some managers fail in integrity. Ironically Bangalore office is called the "Integrity campus". Some managers are gonna be late for the meetings, they don't understand everyone's time is valuable, they randomly slide into the conversation, they "phone call" you randomly, and the work is never done the way it's supposed to be. Sorry to say this. However, on the other side, I've also seen some managers doing great, how they take their team culture seriously. I appreciate that. I believe the manager layer should have certain common traits that all managers should have to create an equal experience for the employees. 

Hybrid policy: Titan has to believe that "best work can be done anywhere" and launch a flexible work policy.

No proper designation - Designation is not defined properly. Only 
the people who defined it will understand what it means kind of designation is what you get. It's perfect in the business teams but not on the other side. 

2. Products/tools used internally - I doubt if the leadership actually reviews or knows the tools teams are using and how it's helping employees in getting work done. Titan is slow in adapting to technology even internal tools that employees use on a daily basis. Most managers or heads would never know about the quality of modern tools and applications and their uses. They often prefer/suggest old-school techniques. (I heard someone saying, "why do you put down things on tool/paper when you can remember everything in your brain". Are you serious? We are all working on multiple projects and brands, and you expect us to remember every task in our brain) While that may work for some, each individual has their own style of working. Titan or any employer has to embrace every working style. That's when a company grows. 

Something that most employees would agree with is the laptop. I think companies prefer to cut costs here because of the number of employees and the tools/products they can invest in. While Titan has the capacity to invest in such tools and laptops, they still prefer to give you laptops that are under-performing for the work you do, tools that do not increase your productivity, and they are gonna block you from using so many websites that they think are harmful. And after increasing complaints, they unblock it. How many times a week does everyone see their laptop not responding to their work? You'll be surprised if we get the numbers. This is also a blocker to effective work. 

3. Pay - It's actually unfair to see people getting higher grades just because of managers, or fewer team members in their team. Whereas you would have contributed much more to the team and company and still you are ranked less and paid less. Why is Titan not identifying and recognizing people who contribute a lot to the company and goes unnoticed? A lot of talents leave the company for this reason, but still, Titan continues to do the same. But the company is performing incredibly well in the market. "When you can increase the salary when someone leaves, why not invest/pay more for the people who stay?" It's a hard truth but everyone agrees that Titan pays no way close to the industry standard may be that's why they are not able to get quality people to the open position. We know it's a people-centric company in long term, but take care of the people who are here short term also. Their contribution does matter to you. Pay based on people's contributions than ranking everyone on the same scale. Who wants that unfair treatment?

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Titan Company Limited is the present name and I started my career way back in 1987 at this esteemed company. It was founded by Tata sons and TIDCO ( Tamil Nadu Industrial Development Corporation ) and Lead by  Late Mr Xerxes Desai (TATAS) and then lead by Mr Bhaskar Bhat the present MD. Stalwarts like Mr Hari Rao added high value to its manufacturing initiative at the Watch division builiding High quality to the products and process.  I have seen the company growing in my own eyes from strength to strength and it rewards you well in terms of Salary and perks. It is highly ethical company to the best of my knowledge. Best company to work for if you are not looking for meteoric rise but for a reasonable growth in your career.  When  you are inside the company, you won't know the value of it that much .., may be because  you are entangled in the day to day affairs. But when you come out and think about your overall experience and the managment, yes it is a company one should work for.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Some of the  continuous  loss making divisions are a drag in the performance and affects the employees and their future of good profit making divisions. This anomaly needs to be rectified.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Working in TITAN gives you a great experience. It has great opportunities to help you grow. Also the company lets you work with several talented employees. They will always push the envelope when it comes to creating new ideas. You can work for long hours that come with a very high level of commitment. The environment at TITAN is always on a change. The people here are so dynamic but there is not much downtime provided.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: You will be expected to work for about 72 hours every week, including the weekends, Mostly Sat & Sun are peak time for us, If you don’t do that and work only fifty hours per week, your manager will not be able to tell you much. Neither will they have a good impression of you. When it comes to your performance review, it could actually turn out pretty bad for you.
